free_text: The 3 MeV Van de Graaff accelerator.
A self-supporting aluminum foil of 150 mu-g/cm**2 in
thickness was used as a target.
detectors were used to detect the alpha-particles.
The energy resolution in the experiment was
Fourteen alpha groups up to 4.3 MeV of energy
levels of the 25Mn nucleus are observed in the
alpha-spectrum. Assuming the Gaussian distribution to
these peaks, the overlapping groups a6 and a7;
a10, a11 and a12; and a13 and a14 have been resolved
by a non-linear least-square fitting technique.
Properties of the energy levels of the 12-MN-25.
sections were obtained by least squire fitting to the
angular distributions with Legendre polynomials and
then integrating over the angles from 90 to 180
degrees and averaging over the deuteron energies from
2.40 to 2.90MeV. The results are normalized to the
